content: Can we be careful adding Clone, Copy to every struct? both the FSP and mmu have a few places where there is definite unnecessary Clone/Copy derives.
Codegen should not generate code if it isn't used (I assume you meant to avoid unoptimized output), but yeah, if they are not necessary I wouldn't want them either
Yes also allowing Copy on structs leads to bad code in certain cases. It's rare you need to pass a struct by value
If you don't have copy people can't fall into the trap
Yes, we've had this discussion in the very early days of Nova already, we should avoid Clone/Copy for larger structures.
... and overall whenever cloning is not strictly necessary. Even for smaller structures, this protects us from potentially sloppy code.

I think we should write this somewhere in the docs or guidelines to make it easier to justify the reasoning when we catch them during reviews. IMO this should be applied to whole kernel not just nova.
